rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DELORES HEARRING
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/26/2024, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 01/29/2024): 1) Notice must be given to the petitioner's former attorney, absent a signed waiver of partial statutory compensation (or a signed apportionment agreement). See CRC 7.704 & Probate Code section 10814. 2) A copy of letters issued to the personal representative of Margaret Johnson's estate and certified within 60 days must be filed conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached. See LRSF 14.35(G) & 14.59(C)(6). 3) Receipts for some tangible personal items have been filed, but such items have not been inventoried and appraised. 4) Was the decedent's 1/5 interest in the Bridgeview Drive property sold for $800,000 x 1/5 = $160,000? 5) Please specify the amount of preliminary distribution each beneficiary received from the estate's 1/5 share of the net proceeds from the sale of the Bridgeview Drive property. 6) Was the decedent's real property in Fresno County sold for $25,000? 7) Is the petitioner waiving statutory compensation for her services as executor? 8) The attorneys' statutory compensation must be ordered to be paid out of the estate, as required by section 10831(d). An allegation must be made as to whether sufficient cash has been advanced to the estate by the petitioner and/or other beneficiaries as the source of payment of the attorneys' compensation. 9) According to the decedent's codicil, Winston Hearring was to receive 2/5 of the estate in the form of a testamentary trust to be administered (and held for 10 years) by the petitioner and Delores Anderson as co-trustees. The petition must be amended to state the terms of the testamentary trust, and the proposed order must be incorporate such terms in compliance with CRC 7.650(b).

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE STEWART LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Judicial Proceedings Concerning Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/26/2024 to be calendared with the related petition for instructions filed by Norman Stewart Jr. The following issues must be adressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the heairng. 3) The petition must state the name and address of each person entitled to notice. See Probate Code section 17201. The petition must also state the names and addresses of all vested and contingent beneficiaries of the trust. See CRC 7.902. 4) The settlors' heirs who are not beneficiaries of the trust, if any, must be served in the manner required by section 17203(b) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE STEWART LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/26/2024. The following issues must be addressed: 1) The petition must state the name and address of each person entitled to notice. See Probate Code section 17201. The petition must also state the names and addresses of all vested and contingent beneficiaries of the trust. See CRC 7.902. 2) According to the petitioner's declaration previously filed (see Exhibit B to the first amended petition filed in this trust matter on 02/22/2021 - see also Attachment 3f(3) to the amended petition for probate of Norman Stewart Sr.'s lost will filed on 07/16/2020 in the related matter PES-20-303558): i) Barbara Stewart and Anassa Stewart were the alternate successor trustees, but section 7.1(b) of the reconstructed trust (Exhibit J to this petition) purports to designate Alfred Stewart as the only alternate successor trustee. ii) Anassa Stewart and Al-Malik Stewart were the remainder beneficiaries of the Lyon Street property, unit #34, but section 6.3(a) of the reconstructed trust (Exhibit J to this petition) purports to designate the petitioner and Alfred Stewart as the remainder beneficiaries of the entire Lyon Street property. iii) Sonja Stewart and Tanis Kidd were the remainder beneficiaries of a portion of the Egbert Avenue property (1235 Egbert Avenue), and Barbara Stewart was the only remainder beneficiary of the remaining portion of the Egbert Avenue property (1225 Egbert Avenue), but section 6.3(b) of the reconstructed trust (Exhibit J to this trust) purports to designate Barbara Stewart as the sole remainder beneficiary of the entire Egbert Avenue property (1225-1235 Egbert Avenue). 3) According to the petitioner's declaration attached to this petition (Exhibit K), if any child of the settlors predeceased them, the child's trust share was to go to the predeceased child's surviving issue, but section 6.3(c) of the reconstructed trust (Exhibit J) purports to authorize direct distribution of the deceased child's share of the designated property to issue if the child failed to survive final distribution, even if the child survived the settlors. Barbara Stewart, one of the settlors' children, survived both settlors. If her interest in the Egbert Avenue property vested, Barbara Stewart's estate (PES-20-303559) must be reopened to receive the Egbert Avenue property from the trust and allocate the same between Sonja Stewart and Tannis Kidd, subject to Treasure Clark's rights under the settlement agreement. 4) Does the petitioner recall reviewing each and every term of the reconstructed trust (Exhibit J)? If not, should the reconstructed terms be limited to identification of the successor trustees and remainder beneficiaries?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHUANG MEI LIU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/3/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proof of service is incomplete at item 4a. (2) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. Please provide a courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/25/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Chung Wen Liu was the devisee pursuant to the decedent's exercise of powers of appointment, but Decedent's will otherwise gifted her entire estate to the Chen Chian Liu and Chuang Mei Liu Living Trust. The other party entitled to notice is the trustee of the devisee trust. Paragraph 4 of the prayer will be disregarded as there were no assets in the estate. If there are after-discovered assets, the estate will need to be reopened.) Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.])

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GITI D. KNOPOFF AND GERALD L. KNOPOFF REVOCABLE TRUST CREATED U/D/T DATED JUNE 27, 2002
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order To Confirm Validity Of Trust, Sole Trustee And Ownership Of Trust Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/16/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An amended petition was e-filed on 2/6/24, which was not at least 30 days before this hearing. (2) A copy of the amended petition was apparently not served with the notice. [Probate Code Section 851(a).] (3) There are still numerous accounts listed on Attachment 3 to the Notice of Hearing, but the amended petition now seeks to have just one account confirmed as trust property. (4) There are minor contingent beneficiaries. A guardian ad litem must be appointed to represent their interests, which are not necessarily the same as their parents'. (5) This Court does not ordinarily order that a trust not otherwise under its supervision is "valid" or that the trustee has the powers granted in the trust. If the trust is being administered by the trustee named in the trust instrument, there is no need for the Court to confirm its validity or the trustee's role and powers. Please provide courtesy copies of the amended petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/5/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (No courtesy copies of the amended petition and papers filed thereafter were received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Lack of courtesy copies can delay file review.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES LEO NEIRINCK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/3/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A person appointed as a fiduciary is required to sign and verify his own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment thereto. [CRC 7.103(c) and Probate Code Section 1023.] (2) Notice is defective if mailed before the pleading requiring notice is e-filed. [LRSF 14.14 A.] The proof of service filed on 2/5/24 shows notice mailed on 2/2/24. The amendment was not e-filed until 2/6/24 (or even uploaded for filing until 2/5/24). (3) The representative capacity of the ADMINISTRATOR is missing in the caption of the pleadings, the notice, and in item 1 of the notice. The administrator's own amendment or his signature and verification of his attorney's amendment must be filed. Notice of the 4/3/24 hearing of the petition and the amendment must be given at least 15 days before the 4/3/24 hearing. (Friends are not parties entitled to notice when there is an heir. A petitioner is not required to give notice to himself. [Probate Code Section 1201.]) Courtesy copies of the administrator's amendment (or his signature and verification of his attorney's amendment) and proof of service of notice of the petition and amendment must be received by the assigned examiner by 3/25/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed. When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 2-1/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SANDRA J. ZRNIC LIVING TRUST DATED JULY 27, 2022
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Assets Of A Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Beverly Bratek, the settlor's sister and heir who is not a beneficiary of the trust, was served on 01/31/2024 when she signed the acknowledgment of receipt (POS-015 form), which was less than 30 days before the previous hearing on 02/13/2024. See CCP section 415.30(c). Moreover, the acknowledgment of receipt only referred to notice of hearing. A copy of the petition was required to be served with the notice. Beverly Bratek must be served in the same manner applicable to a civil summons at least 30 days before the hearing as a person, other than a trustee or beneficiary, whose right, title, or interest would be affected by the petition under Probate Code sections 17203(b) and 851(b)(3). 2) No heirs of Michel Zrnic, the settlor's predeceased spouse, have been identified. Each heir of the predeceased spouse, as a person, other than a trustee or beneficiary, whose right, title, or interest would be affected by the petition, must be served in the manner requred by sections 17203(b) and 851(b)(3). The settlor died within 15 years after her predeceased spouse whose community property interest in real property is alleged to have passed to the settlor. The heirs of the predeceased spouse have standing to contest the settlor's pour-over will if the settlor's probate is opened and, if successful, will take the real property interest that passed from the predeceased spouse under section 6402.5(a). 3) The petition prays for an order confirming 100% ownership of the subject property as an asset of the trust under section 850(a)(3)(B) which provides in relevant part that "where the trustee has a claim to real property, title to which is held by another". According to the preliminary title report attached to the first supplement, however, title to the subject property vests in the petitioner as trustee of the subject trust and the settlor's predeceased spouse. The Court is to determine whether a spousal property petition must be filed in the predeceased spouse's probate (PES) case to confirm the passage of title from the predeceased spouse to the settlor before the settlor signed the deed purporting to transfer 100% ownership of the subject property to her trust, and if so, whether the settlor's probate (PES) case must also be opened to appoint a special administrator or executor to give standing to file the spousal property petition as personal representative of the surviving spouse's estate. See sections 58(a) and 13650(a). 4) Other procedural defects include: i) The second supplement filed on 02/13/2024 was not verified. ii) The declaration attached to the second supplement was not signed in compliance with CCP section 2015.5. iii) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) was not attached to the DE-115 form filed on 02/13/2024. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DAN DEBARDELEBEN TRUST DATED MAY 7, 2013 AS DATED, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Asset
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The petition prays for an order confirming the settlor's 20% interest in real property in Sonoma County. In 2021, the settlor acquired his 20% interest as an unmarried man and tenants in common with the petitioner who acquired her 80% interest as trustee of her own trust. In 2022, the settlor signed a second Addendum to the Trust naming the petitioner as sole trustee and one of the beneficiaries. The settlor died without signing any deed to formally transfer his 20% title to the Sonoma property to his trust. The second Addendum did not list or refer to the Sonoma property. According to the petition: The settlor died intestate, leaving no pour-over will; and the settlor never signed a general assignment of property to the trust. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIA CONEY CORKE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/3/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service has been filed. (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (4) No proof of publication of the notice has been filed. (5) The spouse survived but is shown in item 8 as "Deceased." When did he die? Who is his successor-in-interest if he post-deceased? (6) Bond is required for a non-resident personal representative, notwithstanding the unknown value of the estate. In any case, there is no Attachment 3e(3) attached (waivers of bond by all heirs). (7) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/25/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
